science.title: Alzheimer’s breakthrough: Scientists restore two hours of sleep without clearing brain plaques
science.summary: Researchers have uncovered a surprising culprit behind sleep loss in Alzheimer’s disease: the brain’s own immune cells. In mice with amyloid plaques, overactive microglia triggered inflammation that kept the brain from getting enough deep, restorative sleep. Temporarily removing most of these cells restored more than t

science.title: Quantum breakthrough links light and magnetism in atomically thin materials
science.summary: A new review highlights exciting progress in atomically thin quantum materials where light and magnetism work together in ways never before possible. In these materials, light-generated excitons can interact directly with magnetic behavior, creating opportunities to control magnetic states using light alone. Scientists

science.title: Reusable magnetic invention removes microplastics plus some PFAS from water
science.summary: Microplastics are an increasing global concern, with growing evidence of their presence in water systems. RMIT University researchers have developed a water treatment material that rapidly removes micro- and nanoplastics and some PFAS (per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances), bringing the technology closer to real-world u

science.title: New molecular map reveals how the flu virus hijacks human cells
science.summary: Scientists uncovered how influenza A commandeers human cells, including a surprising strategy that dissolves tiny structures in the nucleus and releases proteins the virus may use to reproduce. The detailed molecular map could reveal new drug targets and help researchers study dangerous flu strains such as H5N1.

science.title: A tiny universe in a bottle reveals clues to the origins of life
science.summary: Researchers have created cosmic dust from scratch by recreating space-like conditions inside glass tubes. The dust contains complex carbon-rich molecules built from elements essential to life and produces infrared signals similar to real material found in space. By studying these laboratory samples, scientists can expl

tech.title: Hyundai claims humanoid robot plan is not part of talks with striking workers
tech.summary: Union previously warned automaker that any robot deployment must be negotiated.

tech.title: Ukrainian drones deliver robots directly into battle by sea and air
tech.summary: Ukraine's battlefield surge of robots now features airdrops and beach assaults.

tech.title: Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
tech.summary: Uber is also investing in Travis Kalanick's company Atoms, which has made gauzy claims about using industrial AI to modernize the world.

tech.title: Science Corporation’s vision-restoring chip wins EU approval
tech.summary: "The thing that the space needs is a company making $100 million a year of revenue," Science Corp. CEO Max Hodak said.

tech.title: iOS code could reportedly let Apple cut off apps when users miss iPhone payments
tech.summary: Code found in an iOS 27 beta would allow Apple to put a financed iPhone in "Restricted Mode" if it detects any missed payments, 9to5Mac reports. The finding follows a story from Bloomberg earlier this week claiming Apple is preparing to launch a new "Apple Upgrade" financing program for leasing new devices. iPhones in

medicine.title: Study shows progress in women's inclusion in clinical trials
medicine.summary: There's good news to report when it comes to women's health. A new study provides encouraging evidence that most clinical trials have substantial female representation relative to disease burden. The study also shows an increase in therapeutic investment for diseases affecting women.

medicine.title: Alzheimer’s breakthrough: Scientists restore two hours of sleep without clearing brain plaques
medicine.summary: Researchers have uncovered a surprising culprit behind sleep loss in Alzheimer’s disease: the brain’s own immune cells. In mice with amyloid plaques, overactive microglia triggered inflammation that kept the brain from getting enough deep, restorative sleep. Temporarily removing most of these cells restored more than t

medicine.title: Scientists discover a protein that protects the brain from Alzheimer’s damage
medicine.summary: A protein called SORLA may act as a powerful defense against the toxic tau tangles involved in Alzheimer’s disease. Mice with extra SORLA had less tau accumulation, less brain atrophy, and healthier connections between neurons, while mice without it experienced more severe damage. The findings also revealed a possible

medicine.title: This drug could help millions keep their kidneys working longer
medicine.summary: An international study found that finerenone slows kidney function decline and reduces the risk of serious kidney and cardiovascular complications in people with chronic kidney disease who do not have diabetes. The results could open the door to a much-needed new treatment option for the majority of CKD patients who ha

medicine.title: AI and sleep analysis could facilitate early diagnosis of Alzheimer's
medicine.summary: Researchers from Universidad Carlos III de Madrid (UC3M) and Hospital Universitario Severo Ochoa in Leganés, part of the public health network of the Comunidad de Madrid, have developed a methodology that uses artificial intelligence (AI) to analyze brain electrical activity during sleep and facilitate the early diagno

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win 2026 Breakthrough, New Horizons prizes
mathematics.summary: Faculty member Shu-Heng Shao, in addition to four MIT alumni, are honored by the Breakthrough Prize Foundation.

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: Seven Perfect Shuffles Randomize a Deck of Cards. But How Many Sloppy Ones?
mathematics.summary: A decades-old proof showed that seven shuffles are enough to mix up a deck of cards. But it requires you to cut the deck with the precision of a professional magician. A new proof gets around that obstacle. The post Seven Perfect Shuffles Randomize a Deck of Cards. But How Many Sloppy Ones? first appeared on Quanta Mag

mathematics.title: How Terry Tao Became an Evangelist for AI in Math
mathematics.summary: With automated proof-checkers, a problem can be broken up into small chunks, solved bit-by-bit, then reassembled with confidence that every piece is correct. For some, this heralds a new area in mathematical research. The post How Terry Tao Became an Evangelist for AI in Math first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up
mathematics.summary: By replacing the most fundamental concept in topology, Peter Scholze and Dustin Clausen are taking the first step in a far bigger program to understand why numbers behave the way they do. The post Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up first appeared on Quanta Magazine

space.title: Robotic Servicing Mission Launches with NASA Support
space.summary: Following its liftoff from Cape Canaveral on July 21 aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket, the Mission Robotic Vehicle (MRV) hosting the NASA-supported Robotic Servicing of Geosynchronous Satellites (RSGS) payload is now en route to geosynchronous Earth orbit, where it will use its advanced robotics to service spacecraft. R

space.title: China's record-breaking Gravity-1 rocket launches 9 satellites from a ship at sea (video)
space.summary: Orienspace's Gravity-1, the world's most powerful solid rocket, aced its third-ever mission on Tuesday (July 21), launching nine satellites to orbit from a ship at sea.

space.title: NASA Sets Coverage for Astronaut Chris Williams, Crewmates Return
space.summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams and Roscosmos cosmonauts Sergey Kud-Sverchkov and Sergei Mikaev are wrapping up their 241‑day mission aboard the International Space Station. The crew and its Soyuz MS-28 spacecraft will undock from the orbiting laboratory’s Rassvet module at 3:02 a.m. EDT Sunday, July 26, heading for a pa

space.title: SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket sends Starlink satellites to orbit a day after rare launch abort
space.summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket carrying 24 Starlink satellites launched from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California on Tuesday (July 21), a day after aborting its first liftoff attempt.

space.title: SpaceX launches satellite repair drone with 10-foot robotic arms to Earth orbit (video)
space.summary: SpaceX launched Northrop Grumman's trailblazing MRV-MEP satellite-servicing mission to Earth orbit today (July 21).

astronomy.title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers the secrets of cosmic 'factories' that filled the early universe with stardust
astronomy.summary: Using the James Webb Space Telescope, astronomers have discovered the secrets of early galaxies that pumped the infant cosmos full of dust, but by studying a much closer and modern galaxy.

astronomy.title: Exoplanet or Exomoon? New Contender Elicits Skepticism
astronomy.summary: Astronomers have found a Jupiter-mass world orbiting a brown dwarf that orbits a star. The post Exoplanet or Exomoon? New Contender Elicits Skepticism appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: "Rogue" Neptunes Are Detached But Not Alone
astronomy.summary: "Rogue" Neptune-size worlds might not roam the galaxy totally on their own, just very far away from their parent stars. The post "Rogue" Neptunes Are Detached But Not Alone appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: The Milky Way's Most Powerful Particle Accelerator
astronomy.summary: Astronomers have conclusively identified a source of the highest energy cosmic ray protons in the Milky Way, solving a long standing puzzle near the star Altair. By combining gamma ray, radio, and X-ray data from three separate observatories, a Hiroshima University led team ruled out competing explanations and confirme

astronomy.title: NASA's Roman Space Telescope could reveal black holes ripping up stars. It's set to launch Aug. 30
astronomy.summary: NASA's next super telescope, the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, may leave nowhere to hide for black holes that violently murder and devour stars, not even cosmic noon.

architecture.title: Made to Measure: Herzog & de Meuron’s Architectural Approach to Furniture Design
architecture.summary: What allows a single architectural principle to generate many different outcomes? The answer lies in the systems that organize architecture: geometry, proportion, structure, and construction. architecture.title: Urban Revivo Sanlitun Flagship Store / MVRDV
architecture.summary: MVRDV-designed Urban Revivo flagship store in Beijing blends local architecture with global aspirations.

architecture.title: MVRDV wraps Beijing roof tiles around Chinese moon gates for fashion flagship store
architecture.summary: Architecture studio MVRDV has designed a flagship store for Chinese fashion brand Urban Revivo in Beijing, China, with a dramatic facade informed by the roof tiles on the city's traditional houses. Located within Beijing's Sanlitun shopping district, the store sits along the main pedestrian route of the district that c

architecture.title: MVRDV wins competition for "new world wonder" in the Netherlands
architecture.summary: Architecture studio MVRDV has been revealed as the winner of social enterprise Shift's competition to design a €240 million landmark in Rotterdam, the Netherlands. The winning proposal, named Rotterdam Rocks!, was selected from a five-strong shortlist that also included Heatherwick Studio, Mecanoo, Office for Political

architecture.title: KPF wraps China skyscraper in one-kilometer-long "landscape ribbon"
architecture.summary: A one-kilometer-long series of terraces and planted staircases wraps around a trio of interconnected buildings in Shenzhen, China, for tech company TP-Link. Designed by architecture studio KPF, TP-Link LXD contains 124,600-square-metres of offices, research and development spaces and housing in three towers. The desire

construction.title: Letter to the Editor: There's Nothing New Under the Sun for Bridges
construction.summary: I get a bit amused by articles on “new” construction methods for bridges, such as the following one in Ohio.

construction.title: Correction: Wrong Casino Collapse Photo
construction.summary: Related Links: Engineering News Record Architectural Record ﻿The story "OSHA Points to Process Errors in Cincinnati Casino Collapse" contained errors. The photograph depicted another Horseshoe Casino, which collapsed last December in Cleveland. Also, Messer Construction Co. received its last OSHA violation in January 2

construction.title: Innovative geotechnical sub-alliance formed for Sizewell C construction
construction.summary: Balfour Beatty Ground Engineering (BBGE), VSL Systems (UK) Ltd (VSL), and Bachy Soletanche (BSL) have formed the Geotechnical Sub-Alliance (GSA) at Sizewell C, believed to be the first of its kind. The post Innovative geotechnical sub-alliance formed for Sizewell C construction appeared first on New Civil Engineer.

construction.title: CECA warns falling workloads should be ‘alarm bell’ for infrastructure delivery
construction.summary: Civil engineering contractors have urged the UK Government to accelerate infrastructure delivery after reporting a fall in workloads during the first quarter of 2026. The post CECA warns falling workloads should be ‘alarm bell’ for infrastructure delivery appeared first on New Civil Engineer.

construction.title: How AI automation can fit into construction workflows: McKinsey
construction.summary: As artificial intelligence continues to shake up the building industry, choosing how to build versus how to buy solutions is important, said an expert for the consulting firm.
